originalTitle: Casey Neistat’s guide to posting every day
summary: Some news: The Vergecast is now a daily podcast! Starting today, we'll be posting every weekday, with even more gadgets and rankings and conversations and feelings and podcasts-within-podcasts. We're excited for all the ways this new schedule lets us tell new kinds of stories, experiment with new tech and new formats, and involve you even […]
content: Some news: The Vergecast is now a daily podcast! Starting today, we’ll be posting every weekday, with even more gadgets and rankings and conversations and feelings and podcasts-within-podcasts. We’re excited for all the ways this new schedule lets us tell new kinds of stories, experiment with new tech and new formats, and involve you even more in the show. (The other immediate change to know about: We’ll be publishing the show in the afternoons going forward, so we can include the day’s biggest news in our 90 Seconds on The Verge segment. The goal is to always be live before 4PM ET. Often well before.) As we’ve been preparing for our new daily schedule, we’ve discovered that posting daily is… a lot of posting. So we sought some advice from a legend of the genre. Casey Neistat posted a video on his YouTube channel for more than 800 days in a row, starting in 2015, and learned a lot along the way. He understands YouTube, and the current creator ecosystem, as well as anyone, so we asked him to share all his thoughts and tips for how to post every day without losing your mind or running out of ideas. (Here’s the video he said took him nine months to get done.) Subscribe: Spotify | Apple Podcasts | Overcast | Pocket Casts | More In addition to the new episodes, we also have new merch! We’ve been working on some cool shirts, mugs, and other stuff for all our shows — if you want to rep Decoder, Version History, or The Vergecast, or you’re just very eager to remind everyone how you feel about Brendan Carr, head to The Verge Shop now. And keep that page bookmarked, because we have a lot of cool stuff coming. If you have thoughts, feedback, or ideas about how we can make this new iteration of The Vergecast even better, we’d love to hear them! Call The Vergecast Hotline at 866-VERGE11 and tell us everything, or send us an email at vergecast@theverge.com. We’re going to do another one of these tomorrow, and the next day, and the next day, and we’re excited to get to do it with you.
chineseTitle: Casey Neistat 的每日发帖指南
chineseSummary: 一些消息：The Vergecast 现在是每日播客！从今天开始，我们将在每个工作日发布更多小工具、排名、对话、感受以及播客中的播客。我们对这个新的时间表让我们讲述新类型的故事、尝试新技术和新格式、甚至让您参与的所有方式感到兴奋 […]
chineseContent: 一些消息：The Vergecast 现在是每日播客！从今天开始，我们将在每个工作日发布更多小工具、排名、对话、感受以及播客中的播客。我们对这个新的时间表让我们讲述新类型的故事、尝试新技术和新格式以及让您更多地参与演出的所有方式感到兴奋。 （另一个需要了解的直接变化是：我们将在下午发布该节目，这样我们就可以在 90 Seconds on The Verge 部分中包含当天的最大新闻。我们的目标是始终在美国东部时间下午 4 点之前直播。通常很早。）当我们一直在为新的每日时间表做准备时，我们发现每天发布的内容是……大量的发布。因此，我们向该流派的传奇人物寻求了一些建议。从 2015 年开始，Casey Neistat 连续 800 多天在他的 YouTube 频道上发布视频，一路上学到了很多东西。他了解 YouTube、当前的创作者生态系统以及任何人，因此我们请他分享他的所有想法和技巧，了解如何每天发帖而不会失去理智或失去创意。 （这是他说他花了九个月才完成的视频。）订阅：Spotify | 苹果播客| 阴天 | 袖珍铸件 | 更多 除了新剧集之外，我们还有新周边！我们一直在为我们所有的节目制作一些很酷的衬衫、马克杯和其他东西——如果你想代表 Decoder、Version History 或 The Vergecast，或者你只是非常渴望提醒大家你对 Brendan Carr 的看法，现在就前往 The Verge Shop。并将该页面添加为书签，因为我们即将推出很多很酷的东西。如果您对我们如何使 The Vergecast 的新版本变得更好有任何想法、反馈或想法，我们很乐意提供
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
